what_it_does: This file is a JSON address registry that lists every valid ui:// address in the system, mapping each address to a UI region, a fixed set of capabilities, the real feature it represents, the source code that powers it, and an operator-level how-to description. It is meant to mirror or extend the running app UI_REGISTRY so mockup elements and real components share one address, and it is extended by adding entries here plus carrying the data-ui-ref in mockups.
observations: Each entry is keyed by a ui:// address and contains region, capabilities, represents, code, and howto fields.
Capabilities are drawn from a small vocabulary (pointable, spotlit, presentable, openable, driven) defined in contracts/ui_info.py, though some entries use a non-enumerated value driven-read-only.
The file documents both coarse regions (toolbar, canvas, inspector, inbox, chat, etc.) and fine-grained controls (ui://toolbar/run, ui://chat/mic, ui://walkthrough/verdict).
The howto field follows a repeated three-part pattern: WHAT, WHAT YOU CAN DO, and HOW TO CHANGE IT, written for an operator rather than a developer.
Some entries include extra metadata such as a roles array or a provenance object noting how the howto was filled.
The top-level _what and scheme fields explain the registry contract and the ui:// and run:// address schemes.
It references parse.py as the tool that refreshes element-map.json after registration changes.
Many code pointers mix frontend TSX files, backend Python modules, and node-type Python files in a single string.
standouts: The registry treats addressability as a first-class design substrate, not just a runtime concern.
Operator-facing how-tos are stored directly in the design ledger alongside code pointers.
A fixed capability taxonomy is attached to each address, enabling generic UI behaviors like pointing, spotlighting, and opening.
Entries can carry provenance metadata, e.g. ui://toolbar/portal notes its howto was filled by rg10-curated-pass.
conventions: Consistent entry schema: region, capabilities, represents, code, howto.
represents values are compact feature tokens such as RUN-run, NODE-types, CAN-compose, RHM-chat.
howto uses the structure WHAT / WHAT YOU CAN DO / HOW TO CHANGE IT.
Lists inside strings are separated by middle dots (\u00b7).
concerns: The file is large and manually edited, so it could drift from the live UI_REGISTRY unless parse.py refresh is reliably run.
Some entries use capability value driven-read-only, which is not listed in the stated capability vocabulary.
The code field is a free-form string mixing multiple source locations, making automated linking fragile.
The provided excerpt is truncated mid-string at ui://settings/roles, so completeness cannot be verified from the excerpt alone.
notes: This is the master address ledger: every pointable UI element gets a stable ui:// name, a capability tag, and a how-to so the interface can be narrated, pointed at, and changed by address.
questions: Is this file the source of truth, or does the live UI_REGISTRY feed back into it?
How are capabilities validated, and should driven-read-only be added to the enumerated capability list?
What is the canonical registry of represents tokens and roles?
How does parse.py detect new mockup elements and ensure their howto fields are populated?
Are the howto fields consumed by runtime help panels, or only by design-time documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is an address registry linking mockup elements to real features and code, and that is exactly what the entries contain.
apparent_themes: One shared identifier for mockup and runtime components
Operator-level documentation embedded in the system ledger
intention_signals: The file is trying to make every piece of the UI surface stable, nameable, and self-describing so that help text, guided tours, build intents, and runtime components can all refer to the same ui:// address and reason about what the element is for.
novelty: It combines a runtime-style address registry with authored, user-altitude how-to guidance and a capability taxonomy, turning a component catalog into a narratable, pointable interface substrate.
contribution: It contributes a stable, human-readable addressing scheme and per-element metadata (region, capabilities, represented feature, code pointer, usage instructions) that can serve as the canonical bridge between UI mockups, runtime components, help panels, walkthroughs, and self-modification intents.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON registry of ui:// addresses for the Company UI, mapping each element to its region, capabilities, represented feature, implementing code, and an operator-level how-to.
intention_for_embedding: To create one addressable source of truth linking every UI element to its real implementation and usage documentation so the system can be pointed at, narrated, and modified by address.
